target.name: euphorikanin A
target.motivation: Bioinspired synthesis to elucidate plausible biogenetic routes for structurally complex natural products.

success_criteria.free_text: Achieve the proposed benzilic acid rearrangement to form the bridged lactone ring.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond in the lactone ring
key_disconnections.reasoning: This rearrangement is proposed to mimic a key biosynthetic step, forming the bridged lactone ring from a precursor with a different ring system.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis aimed to validate a proposed biosynthetic pathway involving a benzilic acid rearrangement to construct the unique bridged lactone core of euphorikanin A. The starting material, (-)-carene, already contains the dimethyl cyclopropane motif, simplifying the initial stages.

thought.background: The target molecule, euphorikanin A, possesses a complex bridged lactone ring system. Biosynthetic proposals suggest a benzilic acid rearrangement as a key step in its formation.
thought.gap: A suitable precursor for the benzilic acid rearrangement needs to be synthesized from a readily available starting material.
thought.decision: To synthesize the crucial biosynthetic precursor (156), (-)-carene (155) was chosen as the starting material and elaborated over 29 linear steps. This precursor contains the necessary structural elements for the subsequent rearrangement.

outcome.observation: Intermediate 156 was obtained after 29 linear steps from (-)-carene (155).

evidence.text_span: The Jia group reported their bioinspired synthesis of euphorikanin A (154) starting from (−)-carene (155), already containing the dimethyl cyclopropane motif [243,244]. Intermediate 156 was reached after 29 linear steps and suggested to be the crucial biosynthetic precursor (protected as its acetate, see Scheme 37A).

thought.background: Intermediate 156, a protected biosynthetic precursor, has been synthesized. The next step is to perform the proposed benzilic acid rearrangement.
thought.gap: The rearrangement requires deprotection of the acetate and subsequent ring contraction.
thought.decision: To achieve the benzilic acid rearrangement, the acetate protecting group was cleaved using a strong alkali, generating an oxyanion (156a) that would undergo the 1,2-alkyl shift to form the bridged lactone ring of euphorikanin A (154).

outcome.observation: Treatment with strong alkali cleaved the ester in 156, revealing oxyanion 156a, which underwent a 1,2-alkyl shift in a benzilic acid rearrangement to give the bridged lactone ring of euphorikanin A (154).

evidence.text_span: Treatment with strong alkali cleaved the ester in 156, revealing oxyanion 156a, which underwent a 1,2-alkyl shift in a benzilic acid rearrangement to give the bridged lactone ring of euphorikanin A (154).

source.title: Ring contraction and ring expansion reactions in terpenoid biosynthesis and their application to total synthesis.
source.authors: Kratena N, Heinzig N, Gärtner P.
